title: The Yellow Robe Monster
content:
Li Ming and his team continued their journey, traveling through forests and mountains. They encountered more demons, but Li Ming was able to defeat them all.

One day, they came to a village that was in ruins. The houses were burned, and the villagers were hiding in fear.

"What happened here?" Tang Sanzang asked.

A villager came out of hiding, his face filled with terror.

"The Yellow Robe Monster attacked us," the villager said. "He took our women and children, and he destroyed our village."

Li Ming's eyes narrowed. "Where is he?"

The villager pointed to a mountain in the distance. "He lives in a cave on that mountain."

***

Li Ming flew to the mountain, followed by his team. They found the cave, and they walked inside.

The cave was dark and foreboding, with torches burning on the walls. They walked through the tunnels, their senses on high alert.

Finally, they came to a large chamber. The Yellow Robe Monster was sitting on a throne, surrounded by his minions. He was wearing a yellow robe, and he had a fierce expression.

"Welcome," the Yellow Robe Monster said. "I've been expecting you."

Li Ming stepped forward. "Yellow Robe Monster, release the villagers!"

The Yellow Robe Monster laughed. "You think I'll release them? They're my prisoners, and they'll stay that way."

He stood up, and Li Ming saw that he was a massive creature, with yellow skin and horns. He was holding a large spear.

"You'll have to fight me first," the Yellow Robe Monster said.

***

Li Ming charged at the Yellow Robe Monster, his golden staff in hand. The Yellow Robe Monster swung his spear, and Li Ming dodged.

They fought for hours, neither of them gaining the upper hand. The Yellow Robe Monster was powerful, but Li Ming was faster and more agile.

Finally, Li Ming saw an opening. He struck the Yellow Robe Monster with his golden staff, hitting him in the chest.

The Yellow Robe Monster fell to the ground, defeated.

"You've won," the Yellow Robe Monster said. "But this isn't over. There are more demons out there, and they won't let you reach the scriptures."

Li Ming nodded. "I know. But I'm ready."

***

Li Ming freed the villagers, and they thanked him profusely.

"Thank you, Great Sage!" one of the villagers said. "You've saved our lives!"

Li Ming smiled. "It's nothing."

As they left the cave, Li Ming thought about what had happened. He had helped the villagers, and that was what mattered.

He knew that there would be more battles ahead, but he was ready. He had his team, his golden staff, and his courage.

The journey to the west was continuing, and Li Ming was determined to see it through.
